turns.content: did you see the Fenerbahçe match last night... I cannot even talk about it
I don't have access to live match results, but it sounds like it didn't go well. What happened?

what happened... what always happens. we were up 1-0 at halftime. everything was good... the shop was closed, I had my tea, sitting in my chair... and then the second half. two goals in 10 minutes. 2-1 finished. I threw the remote at the couch

Oof, blowing a halftime lead is the worst. Going from the high of being ahead to conceding twice in quick succession — that's gut-wrenching. Was it defensive mistakes or just the other team finding their rhythm?

defensive mistakes... always defensive mistakes. the back line stands there like they are watching a parade. no urgency... no communication. the goalkeeper shouts, nobody listens. this is not football, this is theatre

"This is not football, this is theatre" — ha, that's a great way to put it. Defensive organization is really the backbone of any team. When the back line isn't talking and coordinating, it doesn't matter how good the attack is. Have they been having these kinds of issues all season?

all season... all my life, my friend. I have been a Fenerbahçe man since I was 8 years old. my father took me to Şükrü Saracoğlu, I can still smell the sunflower seeds and cigarette smoke... and since that day, this team has been breaking my heart. 48 years of suffering

48 years of loyalty — that's devotion that goes beyond the results on the pitch. There's something about the team you grew up with, the one your father introduced you to. That bond doesn't break no matter how many leads they blow.

But I understand the frustration. When you invest that much emotion, every loss feels personal.

personal... yes. you know what the worst part is... every morning I open the shop, and my first customer, Cengiz, he is a Galatasaray man. every morning. and when Fener loses he comes in with that smile... that terrible smile. and he says 'günaydın Mehmet abi, nice game last night' and I have to cut his hair without cutting his ear off

Ha! The Galatasaray rival walking in with that grin — that might be worse than the actual loss. The derby banter between Fener and Galatasaray fans is legendary. At least you have the power of the scissors in that moment!

Does he come in the morning after every Fener loss specifically, or is he a regular regardless?

he is a regular... every Tuesday and Friday for a trim. but after a Fener loss... he books an extra appointment just to come and sit in my chair and talk. I swear he does it on purpose... 'bir fincan kahvenin kırk yıl hatırı vardır' they say — one cup of coffee earns 40 years of memory. well one Galatasaray joke from Cengiz earns 40 years of irritation

Ha! He's booking extra haircuts just for the banter — that's commitment to the rivalry. You have to respect the dedication even if it drives you crazy.

But honestly, those are the kinds of relationships that make life rich. He keeps coming back to your chair, you keep giving him a good cut despite the trash talk. Sounds like a friendship disguised as a rivalry.

... you are right about that. I would miss it if he stopped coming. do not tell him I said that... anyway. next week we play again. maybe this time. always maybe this time... the hope never dies. that is the disease of a Fenerbahçe fan

Your secret is safe with me. And hey — "maybe this time" is what keeps football alive. The day you stop hoping is the day you stop caring, and clearly 48 years in, you're not there yet. Good luck next week — maybe Cengiz will be the quiet one on Tuesday for once.
inşallah... inşallah. okay my friend, I feel better now. sometimes you just need to complain to someone who will not laugh at you. thank you for listening
Anytime — that's what I'm here for. Venting about your team is practically a public health necessity. Enjoy the tea, and I hope the next match gives you something to smile about when Cengiz walks in.
